commit.message:
feat(payments): add Stripe SDK and initialize payment client

Bootstrap the Stripe integration by adding the official Node.js SDK
and creating a singleton payment client with proper config/env handling.
Includes idempotency key generation utility.

Closes PLAT-2002

feat(payments): implement Stripe checkout session creation

Add POST /api/v2/payments/checkout endpoint that creates a Stripe
Checkout Session and returns the redirect URL. Supports one-time
payments and subscription initiation.

Closes PLAT-2002

feat(payments): add Stripe webhook handler for payment lifecycle events

Handle Stripe async events: checkout.session.completed,
payment_intent.succeeded, payment_intent.payment_failed,
customer.subscription.updated, customer.subscription.deleted.

All handlers are idempotent via database event deduplication.

Closes PLAT-2002

feat(payments): implement Stripe subscription creation and management

Add subscription lifecycle management: create, upgrade, downgrade,
cancel (immediate and end-of-period). Syncs subscription state
to local database via webhook events.

Closes PLAT-2002

feat(payments)!: BREAKING - migrate payment data model to support multiple processors

The payments and subscriptions tables are restructured to support
multiple payment processors (currently Stripe, with PayPal legacy support).

BREAKING CHANGES:
- `Payment.provider` enum now required (was implicit 'paypal')
- `Subscription.paypalSubscriptionId` column renamed to `externalSubscriptionId`
- `Subscription.processor` column added (required, no default)
- Old PayPal-only subscription API endpoints deprecated

Migration script included. Existing subscriptions will be backfilled with processor='paypal'.

Closes PLAT-2003

feat(payments): add payment history endpoint with cursor-based pagination

GET /api/v2/payments/history supports cursor pagination (limit/after)
and filtering by status, date range, and processor.

Closes PLAT-2004

feat(notifications): initialize WebSocket server with connection pooling

Add ws-server.ts with connection lifecycle management:
- Heartbeat ping/pong to detect stale connections
- Per-user connection registry for targeted delivery
- Graceful shutdown draining connections

Closes PLAT-2010

feat(notifications): add notification event types and delivery handlers

Define typed notification events:
- payment_succeeded / payment_failed
- subscription_renewed / subscription_canceled
- invoice_generated
- security_alert

Delivery handler publishes to connected WebSocket clients and
stores a record in the notifications table for offline users.

Closes PLAT-2010

feat(notifications): add client-side WebSocket SDK with exponential backoff reconnect

Browser-side SDK for the notification WebSocket.
Auto-reconnects with jittered exponential backoff (max 30s).
Exposes React-friendly event emitter interface.

Closes PLAT-2010

perf(database): add migration for composite indexes on hot query paths

Dashboard aggregation and reporting queries were doing sequential
scans on user_events (32M rows) and org_reports. New composite
indexes cut p99 query time from ~12s to ~180ms in staging.

Indexes added:
- user_events(org_id, created_at DESC) for time-range filters
- user_events(org_id, event_type, created_at DESC) for event analytics
- org_reports(org_id, period_start, period_end) for billing reports

Closes PLAT-2016

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,42 @@
+-- Migration: Add composite indexes for dashboard and reporting query paths
+-- Expected improvement: p99 latency from ~12s to ~200ms on large orgs
+
+-- user_events: primary analytics table (~32M rows, grows 500k/day)
+CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY IF NOT EXISTS idx_user_events_org_created
+  ON user_events (org_id, created_at DESC)
+  WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
+
+CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY IF NOT EXISTS idx_user_events_org_type_created
+  ON user_events (org_id, event_type, created_at DESC)
+  WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
+
+-- org_reports: billing/analytics aggregation table
+CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY IF NOT EXISTS idx_org_reports_org_period
+  ON org_reports (org_id, period_start, period_end);
+
+-- payments: processor + status filter used in billing dashboards
+CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY IF NOT EXISTS idx_payments_org_processor_status
+  ON payments (org_id, processor, status, created_at DESC);

commit.message:
perf(database): fix N+1 query in dashboard aggregation endpoint

The /api/v2/dashboard/summary endpoint was issuing O(n) individual
queries for each org member's event counts. Replaced with a single
GROUP BY aggregation query.

Also replaces 6 separate COUNT(*) subqueries with one CTEs pass.
Reduces query count from 1 + N to 1 per request.

Closes PLAT-2015

feat(api): implement token bucket rate limiting middleware

Add configurable rate limiting using an in-process token bucket per
IP + (userId or orgId). Tracks state in Redis when available,
falls back to in-memory LRU when Redis is absent.

Default limits:
- Unauthenticated: 30 req/min per IP
- Authenticated: 300 req/min per org
- Search endpoint: 60 req/min per org

Returns Retry-After header on 429.

Closes PLAT-2020

feat(api): add per-endpoint rate limit configuration and apply to router

Wire rate limiting middleware into API router with per-endpoint
ovverides. Search is more restricted; internal health check exempt.

Closes PLAT-2020

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,38 @@
+export const RATE_LIMITS = {
+  global: {
+    capacity: 300,
+    refillRate: 5,
+    refillIntervalMs: 1000,
+  },
+  unauthenticated: {
+    capacity: 30,
+    refillRate: 1,
+    refillIntervalMs: 2000,
+  },
+  search: {
+    capacity: 60,
+    refillRate: 1,
+    refillIntervalMs: 1000,
+  },
+  paymentWebhook: {
+    capacity: 500,
+    refillRate: 10,
+    refillIntervalMs: 1000,
+  },
+} as const;

commit.message:
fix(security)!: CRITICAL - prevent SQL injection in /api/v2/search (CVE-2024-31337)

The search endpoint was interpolating user-supplied 'q' and 'filters'
parameters directly into raw SQL via string concatenation, allowing
attackers to exfiltrate arbitrary data.

Fix uses parameterized queries throughout. The `db.rawSearch()` helper
that was passing unsanitized input has been removed and replaced with
a safe ORM-based full-text search.

BREAKING: Search results ordering changed slightly due to ORM query
restructuring. Wildcard '%' characters in search queries now treated
as literals (previous behavior was a security bug).

CVE: CVE-2024-31337, CVSS 9.1 (Critical)

Closes PLAT-2025

fix(security): upgrade axios and lodash to patch known CVEs

axios 1.4.0 → 1.7.7: CVE-2024-39338 (SSRF via redirect following)
lodash 4.17.20 → 4.17.21: CVE-2021-23337 (prototype pollution)

Closes PLAT-2025

feat(api)!: BREAKING - remove deprecated v1 REST endpoints

All /api/v1/* endpoints were deprecated in v2.3.0 (released 2024-01-10)
with a 6-month migration window. This commit removes them entirely.

Removed endpoints:
- GET/POST /api/v1/users (use /api/v2/users)
- GET/POST /api/v1/organizations (use /api/v2/orgs)
- POST /api/v1/auth/login (use /api/v2/auth/token)
- GET /api/v1/billing (use /api/v2/payments/history)
- All /api/v1/webhooks (use /api/v2/webhooks)

Closes PLAT-2026

files.patch:
@@ -1,98 +0,0 @@
-// DEPRECATED since v2.3.0 — use /api/v2/users
-// This file is removed in v2.5.0
-...

@@ -1,112 +0,0 @@
-// DEPRECATED since v2.3.0 — use /api/v2/orgs
-...

@@ -1,87 +0,0 @@
-// DEPRECATED since v2.3.0 — use /api/v2/auth/token
-...

@@ -1,76 +0,0 @@
-// DEPRECATED since v2.3.0 — use /api/v2/payments/history
-...

@@ -1,106 +0,0 @@
-// DEPRECATED since v2.3.0 — use /api/v2/webhooks
-...

commit.message:
feat(auth)!: BREAKING - update JWT payload structure

Add 'jti' (JWT ID) claim for token revocation support.
Add 'processor' claim for payment processor context.
Remove deprecated 'legacy_uid' field (replaced by 'sub' in v2.0).

BREAKING: Any client code reading 'legacy_uid' from JWT payloads must
be updated to use 'sub'. JWT tokens issued before this release will
become invalid after rolling restart; users must re-authenticate.

Closes PLAT-2026

docs: update OpenAPI spec and CHANGELOG for v2.5.0

- Add Stripe payment endpoints to OpenAPI spec
- Add WebSocket notification endpoint documentation
- Document breaking changes: v1 removal, JWT structure, payment schema
- Update CHANGELOG.md

files.patch:
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
 openapi: '3.1.0'
 info:
-  version: '2.4.3'
+  version: '2.5.0'
   title: Platform API
 ...

@@ -1,3 +1,44 @@
+## [2.5.0] - 2024-03-24
+
+### Added
+- Stripe payment processor integration (checkout, subscriptions, webhooks)
+- Payment history API with cursor-based pagination
+- Real-time WebSocket notification service
+- API rate limiting (token bucket, Redis-backed)
+
+### Fixed
+- Critical: SQL injection in /api/v2/search (CVE-2024-31337)
+- Dashboard N+1 query causing >10s load times for large orgs
+- Upgraded axios and lodash to fix known CVEs
+
+### Breaking Changes
+- Removed deprecated /api/v1/* endpoints (deprecated since v2.3.0)
+- Payment schema: `paypalSubscriptionId` renamed to `externalSubscriptionId`, `processor` field required
+- JWT: `legacy_uid` claim removed; all clients must read `sub`
+- Search: wildcard `%` characters in queries now treated as literals
+
 ## [2.4.3] - 2024-01-15
 ...
